The CC-OFDM MIMO radar waveform designed herein is based on the requirement of surveillance radars used in coastal areas, where the target of interest are ships and small aircrafts in the area within 180 km from the radar site. OFDM signals with subcarrier spacing of 10 KHz will give a maximum unambiguous range of 15 km. reference: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) , is a variation of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) that has been successfully applied to various Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks. LSTM has the ability to capture long-range dependencies with sequences making it suitable for solving sequence labeling problems , , , such as Named Entity Recognition tagging (POS-Tag) and Neural (NER), Part-of-Speech combining (NMT). Study Machine Translation Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) model with Conditional Random Field (CRF) to solve NER and POS-Tag Task. reference: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

In a forensic setting, analysing a large number of log entries resulting from binary anomaly detection is considered inefficient and labour-intensive as the detection is performed on the acquired evidence at one time . Therefore, too many detected anomalies need to be detailed into a more fine- grained detection to help the investigator better prioritise the analysis objective. As a result, this study extends the binary anomaly detection to multi-class anomaly detection, as exemplified by another domain application such as plane flight data , image data , and internet of vehicle . CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORK In this paper, we demonstrate how to train a log-based anomaly detection model that can prioritise the prediction of higher severity anomalies on drone flight logs while increasing the prediction confidence score at the same time. Considering the nature of the dataset, where samples that belong to different severity levels share common features, a multitask vector label representation along with severity-oriented decoding is proposed. An extensive experiment proved that the proposed approach is better than the previous work baselines while being inferior to our baseline scenario based on the accuracy and F1

reference: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
